anchor.snippet: An integer is even if it is divisible by 2, and odd if it is not.

annotations.label: Examples of even and odd numbers

anchor.snippet: For example, −4, 0, and 82 are even numbers, while −3, 5, and 23 are odd numbers.
In particular, the parity of zero is even.
Any two consecutive integers have opposite parity.
An integer expressed in the decimal numeral system is even or odd according to whether its last digit is even or odd.

annotations.label: Parity in binary and odd bases

anchor.snippet: any integer expressed in the binary numeral system is odd if its last digit is 1; and it is even if its last digit is 0
An even number is an integer of the form
An equivalent definition is that an even number is divisible by 2

annotations.label: Set-theoretic definition of even and odd

anchor.snippet: The sets of even and odd numbers can be defined as following
Parity can then be defined as the unique ring homomorphism
even ± even = even
even × even = even
the structure ({even, odd}, +, ×) is in fact the field with two elements
when the quotient is an integer, it will be even if and only if the dividend has more factors of two than the divisor

anchor.section: Higher dimensions and more general classes of numbers
anchor.snippet: Integer coordinates of points in Euclidean spaces of two or more dimensions also have a parity, usually defined as the parity of the sum of the coordinates.

anchor.section: Higher dimensions and more general classes of numbers
anchor.snippet: This form of parity was famously used to solve the mutilated chessboard problem

anchor.section: Higher dimensions and more general classes of numbers
anchor.snippet: The parity of an ordinal number may be defined to be even if the number is a limit ordinal

anchor.section: Higher dimensions and more general classes of numbers
anchor.snippet: Let R be a commutative ring and let I be an ideal of R whose index is 2.

anchor.section: Higher dimensions and more general classes of numbers
anchor.snippet: As an example, let R = Z (2) be the localization of Z at the prime ideal (2).
The even numbers form an ideal in the ring of integers
All prime numbers are odd, with one exception: the prime number 2.
All known perfect numbers are even; it is unknown whether any odd perfect numbers exist.
Goldbach's conjecture states that every even integer greater than 2 can be represented as a sum of two prime numbers.
The parity of a permutation (as defined in abstract algebra ) is the parity of the number of transpositions into which the permutation can be decomposed.
It can be shown that no permutation can be decomposed both in an even and in an odd number of transpositions.
The Feit–Thompson theorem states that a finite group is always solvable if its order is an odd number.
The parity of a function describes how its values change when its arguments are exchanged with their negations.
The Taylor series of an even function contains only terms whose exponent is an even number
an evil number is a number that has an even number of 1's in its binary representation , and an odious number is a number that has an odd number of 1's in its binary representation
The parity function maps a number to the number of 1's in its binary representation, modulo 2
The Thue–Morse sequence , an infinite sequence of 0's and 1's, has a 0 in position i when i is evil, and a 1 in that position when i is odious.
a parity bit appended to a binary number provides the simplest form of error detecting code

annotations.label: Odd harmonics in cylindrical wind instruments

anchor.snippet: In wind instruments with a cylindrical bore and in effect closed at one end, such as the clarinet at the mouthpiece, the harmonics produced are odd multiples of the fundamental frequency
